description: Publicly accessible Next.js web application for UK buy-to-let investors evaluating London residential property investments. Combines real HM Land Registry transactions, ONS indices, police crime data, and EPC Register attributes into a composite investment score, comparables analysis, mortgage scenario modelling, and multi-level historical drill-down. Built for QMUL IOT552U 002 Project Report (70% of module grade). Target mark 95-99/100. Stack: Next.js 15 App Router, TypeScript, Drizzle ORM, Supabase Postgres (London region), tRPC, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, Tremor + Nivo charts. Deployed to Vercel. Ralph handles all coding, infrastructure, deployment, and automated verification. The user writes the 3000-word report and 9 supporting Markdown docs afterward — Ralph does NOT write assessed prose; Ralph creates empty skeletons with required section headings only. Full context in ralph/context/ (6 files): 01-assessment-brief, 02-lecture-synthesis, 03-technical-plan, 04-evidence-requirements, 05-verification-scripts, 06-report-artefacts.

userStories.title: US-001: Project Scaffold, Infrastructure, and Report Artefact Skeletons
userStories.description: As the developer, I want a scaffolded T3 app wired to Supabase and Vercel, CI configured, and all report artefact skeletons produced, so that subsequent stories can focus on code.
acceptanceCriteria: Prerequisite: ~/personal/yieldlens/.secrets/db.env must exist and be readable. If missing, fail fast with message 'Missing secrets file — see ralph/context/05-verification-scripts.md'
Working directory is ~/personal/yieldlens (create via mkdir -p if missing)
Create file .npmrc at repo root containing exactly: registry=https://registry.npmjs.org/ (overrides Amazon CodeArtifact)
Run: pnpm create t3-app@latest . --noGit --CI --tailwind --trpc --drizzle --dbProvider postgres --appRouter --eslint
Verify scaffold by checking these files exist: package.json, tsconfig.json, drizzle.config.ts, src/env.js, src/server/db/schema.ts, src/server/api/trpc.ts, .env, .env.example
git init, then set REPO-SCOPED (not global) git config: git config user.email 'harryfgoatcher@gmail.com' AND git config user.name 'Harry Goatcher'
Append to .gitignore: .secrets/, evidence/**/*.png, evidence/**/*.mp4, *.log, test-results/, playwright-report/, .next/, .vercel/
Install runtime deps: pnpm add @tremor/react @nivo/core @nivo/scatterplot @nivo/geo lucide-react react-hook-form @hookform/resolvers date-fns csv-parse postgres pg

Install dev deps: pnpm add -D vitest @vitest/ui @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom jsdom @axe-core/playwright @playwright/test playwright lighthouse chrome-launcher eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y tsx @types/pg sharp

Add these pnpm scripts to package.json (content in ralph/context/05-verification-scripts.md): dev, build, start, lint (with --max-warnings=0), typecheck, test, test:watch, db:generate, db:migrate, db:push, db:studio, ingest:all, ingest:postcodes, ingest:land-registry, ingest:ons-hpi, ingest:ons-rental, ingest:police, ingest:epc, ingest:mortgages, benchmark, screenshots, audit:a11y, audit:lighthouse, audit:all, verify:data-quality, verify:evidence, verify:references, verify:all

Initialise shadcn/ui non-interactively by writing components.json with style=new-york, rsc=true, tsx=true, tailwind config path, baseColor=slate, cssVariables=true, components alias ~/components, utils alias ~/lib/utils
Add shadcn components: pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add button input form card select table tabs tooltip skeleton alert dialog badge separator — all must succeed

Copy 7 keys from ~/personal/yieldlens/.secrets/db.env into .env at repo root: DATABASE_URL, DIRECT_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF, SESSION_POOLER_URL (Supabase project ref: scuggrmjkrrvciblekjj, org: axvcxtqefcjvbiycylwn, West Europe London — already provisioned)
Verify: test -f .env && grep -c DATABASE_URL .env equals 1
Write .env.example with same 7 keys but placeholder values

Update src/env.js: add server-side zod validation for DATABASE_URL (url), DIRECT_URL (url), SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY (min 20 chars), SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF (min 10). Add client-side for NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL (url), NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY (min 20)
Create scripts/verify-db-connection.ts that connects via DIRECT_URL and runs SELECT 1. Run: pnpm tsx scripts/verify-db-connection.ts — must exit 0

Create GitHub repo: gh repo create harryfgx/yieldlens --public --description 'London BTL investment decision-support web app — QMUL IOT552U project' --license mit --source=. --remote=origin — must succeed
First commit: git add -A && git commit -m 'feat(infra): scaffold T3 app with Supabase and Vercel infrastructure' — verify git log --oneline shows ≥ 1 commit
git push -u origin main — must succeed
Link Vercel: vercel link --yes --project yieldlens — must succeed. Verify .vercel/project.json exists
Add env vars to Vercel in production, preview, and development scopes for all keys in .env. Verify: vercel env ls lists DATABASE_URL in all 3 scopes
Deploy to Vercel production: vercel --prod --yes — must return a URL. Capture URL as PRODUCTION_URL in .env and in Vercel env vars (production scope)
Verify deployment live: curl -sI $PRODUCTION_URL must return HTTP 200 or 1.1 200
Create .github/workflows/ci.yml with jobs: lint, typecheck, test, build — triggered on push to main and pull_request
Push CI workflow; sleep 60 seconds; verify CI run: gh run list --workflow ci.yml --limit 1 --json conclusion --jq '.[0].conclusion' must equal 'success'

Set repo topics: gh repo edit harryfgx/yieldlens --add-topic postgresql --add-topic nextjs --add-topic typescript --add-topic real-estate --add-topic data-analysis --add-topic uk --add-topic supabase --add-topic drizzle

Create evidence/ directory structure with 9 subdirectories: mkdir -p evidence/{01-erd,02-schema-ddl/migrations,03-data-sources/ingestion-logs,04-data-quality,05-sql-queries,06-dashboard,07-migrations-demo,08-deployment,10-references}. Verify: ls evidence/ shows 9 directories

Create evidence/HUMAN_TODO.md listing exactly these 5 items: (1) Supabase Studio schema visualiser screenshot → evidence/01-erd/supabase-studio-schema.png; (2) Vercel dashboard screenshot → evidence/08-deployment/vercel-deployment.png; (3) Supabase project overview screenshot → evidence/08-deployment/supabase-project.png; (4) Record 4-minute Loom walkthrough using evidence/06-dashboard/loom-script.md — paste URL into README.md; (5) Review and submit the 3000-word report

Create empty skeleton files (each with required section headings but no assessed prose) at docs/research-questions.md, docs/scenario-swot.md, docs/user-journey-map.md, docs/risk-register.md, docs/requirements-analysis.md, docs/data-model.md, docs/analytical-queries.md, docs/deployment-plan.md, docs/references.md. Each file must contain the exact headings specified in ralph/context/06-report-artefacts.md for its section. Verify: ls docs/*.md must show 9 files; grep -c '^##' docs/scenario-swot.md ≥ 1; grep -c '^##' docs/data-model.md ≥ 6 (Modelling Approach, Entity Catalogue, Functional Dependency Analysis, Normalisation Walkthrough, Controlled Denormalisation, Indexing Strategy)

Create docs/AGENTS.md (factual operational notes — Ralph writes this one): registry override rule, git identity override rule, Supabase project ref, DATABASE_URL vs DIRECT_URL usage, ingestion + matview refresh procedure

Create README.md at repo root with these sections: Title+Tagline+Live Demo URL+GitHub Link, Problem Statement (≥100 words), Feature List, Tech Stack Table, Data Sources with OGL v3.0 attribution statement, Setup Instructions (pnpm install, .env template, pnpm db:push, pnpm ingest:all, pnpm dev), Project Structure Tree, Licence (MIT), Acknowledgements (QMUL IOT552U, Jonathan Jackson, Seth Zenz, Richard Gunton, data sources)
Unit tests: create tests/setup/smoke.test.ts with exactly one test that imports src/env.js and asserts env parses successfully — mocking process.env with required keys so no network needed
Verify: pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm build — all exit 0
Final commit: git add -A && git commit -m 'feat(infra): complete scaffold with CI, evidence skeleton, and doc skeletons' && git push

Final state verification: ls docs/ shows 10 files (9 skeletons + AGENTS.md); ls evidence/ shows 9 directories + HUMAN_TODO.md; gh repo view --json url --jq '.url' returns https://github.com/harryfgx/yieldlens; curl -sI $PRODUCTION_URL returns 200
Typecheck passes: pnpm typecheck

userStories.title: US-002: Drizzle Schema with 8 Entities and Advanced Postgres Features
userStories.description: As the developer, I want the full 8-entity schema with triggers, stored function, materialised view, and least-privilege role, so that the database satisfies 3NF/BCNF with the A+ 'beyond taught' criterion.
acceptanceCriteria: Precondition: US-001 done (scripts/verify-db-connection.ts exits 0; latest git log shows US-001 final commit)

Define 5 pgEnums in src/server/db/schema.ts: property_type_enum (DETACHED/SEMI_DETACHED/TERRACED/FLAT/OTHER), tenure_enum (FREEHOLD/LEASEHOLD/SHARED_OWNERSHIP), transaction_category_enum (STANDARD_SALE/NEW_BUILD/OTHER), mortgage_type_enum (INTEREST_ONLY/REPAYMENT), verdict_enum (CASH_COW/GROWTH_PLAY/UNICORN/AVOID)

Define table 'location' with columns exactly as in ralph/context/03-technical-plan.md: location_id serial PK, postcode varchar unique indexed, outcode varchar indexed, borough varchar, region varchar, lsoa_code varchar, lat numeric(9,6), lng numeric(9,6), created_at, updated_at

Define table 'property' with: property_id PK serial, uprn bigint unique, location_id FK → location, address_line_1, address_line_2, city, property_type (pgEnum), tenure (pgEnum), bedrooms smallint with CHECK (bedrooms BETWEEN 0 AND 20), floor_area_sqm numeric(8,2), epc_rating, epc_score, construction_age_band, created_at, updated_at

Define table 'transaction': transaction_id PK serial, property_id FK → property, outcode varchar (documented in JSDoc as controlled denormalisation), sale_price numeric(12,2) with CHECK (sale_price > 0 AND sale_price < 100000000), sale_date date with CHECK (sale_date <= CURRENT_DATE), transaction_category (pgEnum), created_at. Composite index idx_transaction_outcode_date on (outcode, sale_date DESC)

Define table 'rental_index': rental_index_id PK, region, reference_month, index_value numeric(8,2), monthly_pct_change numeric(5,2), annual_pct_change numeric(5,2). Unique constraint (region, reference_month)

Define table 'hpi_index': hpi_index_id PK, outcode, reference_month, average_price numeric(12,2), index_value numeric(8,2), annual_pct_change numeric(5,2). Unique constraint (outcode, reference_month). Index idx_hpi_outcode_date on (outcode, reference_month DESC)
Define table 'crime_stat': crime_stat_id PK, lsoa_code indexed, reference_month, category varchar, count integer with CHECK (count >= 0). Unique constraint (lsoa_code, reference_month, category)

Define table 'mortgage_product': mortgage_product_id PK, lender, product_name, mortgage_type (pgEnum), max_ltv_pct numeric(5,2), initial_rate_pct numeric(5,3), initial_period_months smallint, follow_on_rate_pct numeric(5,3), product_fee_gbp numeric(8,2), captured_at date, source_url

Define table 'investor_scenario': scenario_id uuid PK default gen_random_uuid(), shareable_slug varchar unique, target_postcode, asking_price numeric(12,2), expected_monthly_rent numeric(10,2), deposit_pct numeric(5,2), mortgage_product_id FK → mortgage_product, composite_score numeric(5,2), verdict (pgEnum), created_at
Every table has JSDoc comment above its definition describing purpose + (for transaction.outcode) controlled denormalisation justification
Run pnpm db:generate — verify: ls drizzle/*.sql returns ≥ 1 file

Create drizzle/0001_triggers_and_functions.sql: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION trigger_set_timestamp() RETURNS TRIGGER setting NEW.updated_at = NOW(); CREATE TRIGGER set_timestamp_<table> BEFORE UPDATE on location and property

Create drizzle/0002_mortgage_function.sql defining calculate_monthly_payment(principal numeric, annual_rate_pct numeric, term_years integer, mortgage_type text) RETURNS numeric LANGUAGE plpgsql IMMUTABLE. For REPAYMENT with rate=0 return principal/(term_years*12); else amortisation formula principal*r*power(1+r,n)/(power(1+r,n)-1) where r=annual_rate_pct/100/12, n=term_years*12. For INTEREST_ONLY return principal*annual_rate_pct/100/12. If principal=0 return 0
Create drizzle/0003_outcode_trigger.sql: trigger maintain_transaction_outcode BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE on transaction that joins via property → location and sets NEW.outcode

Create drizzle/0004_materialised_view.sql: CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW mv_postcode_investment_metrics with columns outcode, median_sale_price_12m, avg_yield_pct, yoy_price_growth_pct, three_year_price_growth_pct, price_volatility_score, crime_rate_index, yield_quartile (NTILE 4), growth_quartile (NTILE 4), quadrant (CASE returning CASH_COW/GROWTH_PLAY/UNICORN/AVOID). CREATE UNIQUE INDEX on (outcode). CREATE FUNCTION refresh_postcode_metrics() that runs REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY

Create drizzle/0005_readonly_role.sql: CREATE ROLE IF NOT EXISTS app_readonly NOLOGIN; GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA public; GRANT SELECT ON ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA public; GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION calculate_monthly_payment; ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES ... GRANT SELECT ON TABLES
Apply: pnpm db:push — must exit 0. If materialised view fails on empty tables, defer refresh until after ingestion (documented in docs/AGENTS.md)

Write scripts/verify-schema.ts asserting: 8 tables in information_schema.tables; 3 functions (trigger_set_timestamp, calculate_monthly_payment, refresh_postcode_metrics); 1 matview; 1 role. Script exits 0 only if all pass. Run pnpm tsx scripts/verify-schema.ts — must exit 0

Write DBML source at evidence/01-erd/yieldlens-erd-uml.dbml using UML multiplicity notation (e.g., '1..*' on relationship lines). Must contain all 8 tables and 6 FK relationships. Verify: grep -c '^Table ' evidence/01-erd/yieldlens-erd-uml.dbml equals 8 AND grep -c '^Ref:' equals 6
Write evidence/02-schema-ddl/schema-full.sql by concatenating all drizzle/*.sql files. Verify file size > 5000 bytes
Copy individual migrations to evidence/02-schema-ddl/migrations/ — verify file count matches drizzle/ count
Create evidence/02-schema-ddl/advanced-features.sql containing only: mortgage function + outcode trigger + matview DDL + role grants. Verify file size > 1500 bytes

Unit tests: tests/db/mortgage-function.test.ts using postgres.js connection via DIRECT_URL. Test 1: calculate_monthly_payment(300000, 5.5, 25, 'REPAYMENT') returns between 1841 and 1843 (abs diff ≤ 1). Test 2: calculate_monthly_payment(300000, 5.5, 25, 'INTEREST_ONLY') returns exactly 1375.00. Test 3: calculate_monthly_payment(300000, 0, 25, 'REPAYMENT') returns exactly 1000.00. Test 4: calculate_monthly_payment(0, 5.5, 25, 'REPAYMENT') returns exactly 0. Test 5: calculate_monthly_payment(500000, 4.25, 30, 'REPAYMENT') returns between 2459 and 2461

Populate docs/data-model.md FACTUAL CONTENT ONLY (Entity Catalogue + Indexing Strategy sections) — the narrative sections (Modelling Approach, FD Analysis, Normalisation Walkthrough, Controlled Denormalisation) remain as skeletons with required headings for user to fill in. Factual sections must list all 8 entities with attribute catalogue + type + PK/FK flags, and table of every index with name, columns, justification. Verify: grep -c '^## ' docs/data-model.md ≥ 6; grep -c '^### ' docs/data-model.md ≥ 8 (one entity subsection each)
Verify: pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm build — all exit 0
Commit: git add -A && git commit -m 'feat(db): add 8-entity schema with triggers, stored function, materialised view, and BCNF-ready normalisation' && git push
Verify CI green: gh run list --workflow ci.yml --limit 1 --json conclusion --jq '.[0].conclusion' returns 'success'
Typecheck passes: pnpm typecheck

userStories.title: US-003: Data Ingestion Pipeline with DAMA Quality Verification
userStories.description: As the developer, I want ETL scripts that load real London data from 7 sources with programmatic DAMA quality checks, so that the dashboard has authentic verified data.
acceptanceCriteria: Precondition: US-002 done (pnpm tsx scripts/verify-schema.ts exits 0)

Create scripts/ingest/sources.ts exporting: ONS_POSTCODE_DIRECTORY_URL, LAND_REGISTRY_PPD_URL, ONS_HPI_API_BASE (https://landregistry.data.gov.uk/data/ukhpi/region), ONS_RENTAL_API_URL, POLICE_UK_API_BASE (https://data.police.uk/api), EPC_API_BASE (https://epc.opendatacommunities.org/api/v1). Plus LONDON_OUTCODE_PREFIXES array including inner (E, EC, N, NW, SE, SW, W, WC) and outer London (BR, CR, DA, EN, HA, IG, KT, RM, SM, TW, UB, WD)
Create scripts/ingest/lib/db.ts exporting postgres.js client connected via DIRECT_URL (bulk writes need direct connection)
Create scripts/ingest/lib/logger.ts with structured JSON-line logger writing to stdout AND evidence/03-data-sources/ingestion-logs/<script>-<timestamp>.jsonl

scripts/ingest/postcode-directory.ts: stream-download ONS Postcode Directory CSV, filter to London outcodes, insert into location with ON CONFLICT (postcode) DO UPDATE. Must insert ≥ 100000 rows. Log row count

scripts/ingest/land-registry.ts: stream-download Price Paid Complete CSV, filter to London postcodes (match against location.postcode preloaded Set), skip rows with: sale_price <= 0 OR >= 100000000, sale_date > CURRENT_DATE, null postcode. For valid rows: upsert property by (postcode + address); insert transaction. Batch inserts 1000 rows. Log counts: total/valid/skipped_* reasons/duplicates/inserted_properties/inserted_transactions. Must insert ≥ 500000 transactions and ≥ 100000 properties
scripts/ingest/ons-hpi.ts: fetch ONS HPI data for London outcodes + regions, insert into hpi_index with ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE. Must insert ≥ 500 rows covering ≥ 12 months
scripts/ingest/ons-rental.ts: fetch ONS Private Rental Prices Index, insert into rental_index with ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE. Must insert ≥ 500 rows

scripts/ingest/police-crime.ts: call https://data.police.uk/api/crimes-street/all-crime for Metropolitan + City of London forces, last 36 months, grouped by LSOA. Insert into crime_stat with ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE. Handle 429 with exponential backoff (3 retries, 2s base). Must insert ≥ 10000 rows

scripts/ingest/epc.ts: read EPC_API_KEY from env. If missing, log warning and skip gracefully (don't fail). If present: fetch domestic certificates for London local authorities, UPDATE property SET bedrooms/floor_area_sqm/epc_rating/epc_score/construction_age_band WHERE uprn matches

scripts/ingest/mortgage-products.ts: hardcoded TypeScript array of 15 real BTL products with fields lender, product_name, mortgage_type, max_ltv_pct, initial_rate_pct, initial_period_months, follow_on_rate_pct, product_fee_gbp, captured_at (today's date), source_url. Sources: The Mortgage Works (3+), Paragon (3+), Kent Reliance (2+), BM Solutions (2+), Landbay (2+), Fleet (3+). Insert all 15 with ON CONFLICT (lender, product_name) DO UPDATE

scripts/ingest/run-all.ts: execute in dependency order — postcode-directory first (serial); then land-registry + mortgage-products parallel; then ons-hpi + ons-rental + police-crime + epc parallel. Finally: SELECT refresh_postcode_metrics(). Log total duration + per-script row counts
Run pnpm ingest:all — must complete. If Supabase 500MB approach, reduce land-registry to last 5 years (still meets ≥ 500K threshold)

Write scripts/verify-data-quality.ts per ralph/context/05-verification-scripts.md. Must assert 11 threshold queries: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM location ≥ 100000; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM property ≥ 100000; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM transaction ≥ 500000; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM rental_index ≥ 500; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM hpi_index ≥ 500; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM crime_stat ≥ 10000; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM mortgage_product = 15; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM transaction WHERE sale_price <=0 OR >=1e8 = 0; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM transaction WHERE sale_date > CURRENT_DATE = 0; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM property WHERE bedrooms <0 OR >20 = 0; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM crime_stat WHERE count <0 = 0. Writes evidence/04-data-quality/quality-check-results.json with {dimension, metric, value, threshold, pass} per check. Exits 0 only if all 11 pass
Run pnpm verify:data-quality — must exit 0

Write evidence/03-data-sources/source-catalogue.md (factual Markdown table — Ralph writes this one) with columns: Source | URL | Licence | Volume Loaded | Refresh Cadence | Last Captured. Minimum 7 rows (one per source). All gov.uk/ONS/police rows show 'Open Government Licence v3.0'

Populate evidence/04-data-quality/dama-assessment.md FACTUAL CONTENT ONLY — table of 6 DAMA dimensions (Accuracy, Completeness, Consistency, Timeliness, Validity, Uniqueness) with metric + numeric value from quality-check-results.json. Narrative prose sections remain as skeletons with section headings (user writes). Verify: grep -c '^## ' evidence/04-data-quality/dama-assessment.md ≥ 6

Unit tests: tests/ingest/land-registry.test.ts with vitest. Mock csv-parse. Test cases: (1) valid row produces property+transaction inserts; (2) sale_price=0 skipped with reason=invalid_price; (3) sale_price=2e8 skipped with reason=invalid_price; (4) sale_date in future skipped with reason=future_date; (5) null postcode skipped with reason=null_postcode; (6) duplicate row inserts once. Also tests/ingest/run-all.test.ts verifies postcode-directory runs before land-registry

Write docs/data-ingestion.md describing each source, refresh procedure, EPC_API_KEY optionality, known quality issues (Land Registry 1-3 month lag), how to re-run — this is operational doc, Ralph writes it
Verify: pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm build — all exit 0
Commit: git add -A && git commit -m 'feat(ingest): add ETL pipeline with DAMA data quality verification' && git push
Verify CI green
Typecheck passes: pnpm typecheck

userStories.title: US-004: Four Analytical SQL Queries via tRPC, Benchmarked
userStories.description: As the developer, I want 4 multi-table analytical queries exposed via tRPC with captured EXPLAIN plans and performance benchmarks, so that the dashboard has decision-supporting analytics.
acceptanceCriteria: Precondition: US-003 done (pnpm verify:data-quality exits 0)
Create src/server/db/index.ts exporting: db (postgres.js client via DATABASE_URL pooler) for tRPC runtime. Document in JSDoc that production should use app_readonly role connection
Create src/server/api/routers/analytics.ts using tRPC v11 publicProcedure

Implement procedure 'comparables' (Q1, Descriptive + Statistical). Zod input { postcode: z.string().regex(/^[A-Z]{1,2}[0-9R][0-9A-Z]? ?[0-9][A-Z]{2}$/i), bedrooms: z.number().int().min(1).max(10), propertyType: z.enum([...]), floorAreaSqm: z.number().min(10).max(1000) }. SQL uses Drizzle's sql template tag: CTE extracting outcode + floor area bounds ±20%. Main query joins transaction → property → location (3 tables), filters on outcode/bedrooms/propertyType/floor_area_sqm BETWEEN/24-month window. Selects percentile_cont(0.25), percentile_cont(0.5), percentile_cont(0.75), median £/sqft, count. Leading SQL comment: '-- Q1: Comparables Analysis | S11: Descriptive + Statistical | Decision: Is the asking price fair relative to recent local sales?'

Implement procedure 'historicalDrilldown' (Q2, Descriptive + Predictive). Zod input { outcode: z.string() }. SQL uses CTEs + window functions for 4 geographic levels (postcode_sector=LEFT(postcode,4), outcode, area=LEFT(outcode,1 or 2), region='London'): LAG(avg_price, 12) OVER (PARTITION BY geo_level, geo_value ORDER BY month) for YoY; AVG(avg_price) OVER (PARTITION BY geo_level, geo_value ORDER BY month ROWS BETWEEN 11 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) for rolling 12m. Joins transaction + property + location + hpi_index (4 tables). Returns array of {geo_level, geo_value, month, avg_price, rolling_12m_avg, yoy_growth_pct}. Leading comment: '-- Q2: Multi-Level Historical Drill-Down | S11: Descriptive + Predictive | Decision: Is this area accelerating or decelerating vs its wider region?'

Implement procedure 'compositeScore' (Q3 HERO QUERY, Diagnostic). Zod input { postcode, bedrooms, propertyType, askingPrice: number min 50000 max 10000000, expectedMonthlyRent: number min 100 max 20000, depositPct: number min 5 max 50, mortgageProductId: number int positive }. SQL uses multiple CTEs: (1) comparables_cte reusing Q1 logic; (2) growth_cte from hpi_index for outcode; (3) risk_cte from crime_stat aggregated to outcode + price volatility from hpi_index stddev; (4) cashflow_cte calling calculate_monthly_payment(askingPrice*(1-depositPct/100), mp.initial_rate_pct, 25, mp.mortgage_type); (5) score_cte applying NTILE(4) across all London outcodes and weighted: composite = 35*yield_quartile/4 + 25*growth_quartile/4 + 20*risk_quartile/4 + 20*cashflow_quartile/4. CASE classifies verdict: composite>75 AND yield_quartile>=3 AND growth_quartile>=3 = UNICORN; else composite>60 AND yield_quartile>=3 = CASH_COW; else composite>60 AND growth_quartile>=3 = GROWTH_PLAY; else AVOID. Returns { compositeScore, verdict, components: {yieldScore, growthScore, riskScore, cashflowScore}, monthlyPayment, grossYieldPct, netYieldPct }. Joins 6 tables. Leading comment: '-- Q3: Composite Investment Score | S11: Diagnostic | Decision: All factors considered, how good is this BTL investment?'

Implement procedure 'quadrant' (Q4, Descriptive Classification). SQL: SELECT outcode, avg_yield_pct, yoy_price_growth_pct, yield_quartile, growth_quartile, quadrant FROM mv_postcode_investment_metrics ORDER BY outcode. Leading comment: '-- Q4: Yield vs Growth Quadrant Classification | S11: Descriptive Classification | Decision: Which London outcodes suit yield vs growth strategies?'

Implement procedure 'novelInsight' returning median £/sqft difference between top-quartile-lowest-crime outcodes vs bottom-quartile using mv_postcode_investment_metrics + property + transaction. Returns { title, description, value, methodology }. If result insufficient data: returns hardcoded fallback with methodology='fallback' and logs warning — Ralph does NOT silently default

Implement procedure 'compareSnapshot' with Zod input { outcodes: z.array(z.string()).min(1).max(4) }. Returns per-outcode from mv_postcode_investment_metrics: median_price_12m, avg_yield_pct, yoy_growth_pct, crime_rate_index, quadrant
Implement procedure 'listMortgageProducts' returning all mortgage_product rows ordered by lender, product_name
Register analyticsRouter in src/server/api/root.ts under namespace 'analytics'
Copy Q1/Q2/Q3/Q4 SQL with leading comment block to evidence/05-sql-queries/q1-comparables.sql, q2-historical-drilldown.sql, q3-composite-score.sql, q4-quadrant.sql

For each of Q1-Q4: run in psql via DIRECT_URL EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS, FORMAT TEXT) with representative parameters, save plan to evidence/05-sql-queries/q{N}-{name}-explain.txt. Verify each file size > 500 bytes

Create scripts/benchmark.ts per ralph/context/05-verification-scripts.md: connect via DIRECT_URL, warm up with 1 query each, run each of Q1-Q4 100 times with representative params, compute p50/p95/p99, write Markdown table to evidence/06-dashboard/performance-benchmarks.md. Exits 0 iff all p50 < 200ms
Run pnpm benchmark — must exit 0. If any query exceeds p50 200ms, optimise with indexes/rewrite until passes

Unit tests: tests/api/analytics.test.ts using appRouter.createCaller. Tests: (1) comparables with valid input returns { p25, median, p75, median_gbp_per_sqft, comparable_count } object; (2) comparables throws ZodError with error path ['postcode'] on invalid postcode; (3) historicalDrilldown returns array with exactly 4 distinct geo_level values; (4) compositeScore with SE16/2bed/FLAT/400000/1800/25/productId1 returns compositeScore between 0 and 100 AND verdict in {CASH_COW, GROWTH_PLAY, UNICORN, AVOID}; (5) quadrant returns array where every row has quadrant in the 4-enum set; (6) compareSnapshot with 5 outcodes throws ZodError

Populate docs/analytical-queries.md FACTUAL CONTENT ONLY — table of 4 queries with S11 labels, tables joined, SQL features. Leave Loshin BI Spectrum narrative and literature justifications as skeleton sections with required headings (user writes). Verify: grep -c 'S11' docs/analytical-queries.md ≥ 4; grep -c '^## ' docs/analytical-queries.md ≥ 5 (one per query + header)
Verify: pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm build — all exit 0
Commit: git add -A && git commit -m 'feat(api): add 4 analytical tRPC queries with window functions and benchmarks <200ms p50' && git push
Verify CI green
Typecheck passes: pnpm typecheck

userStories.title: US-005: Home Page, Postcode Explorer, and About-the-Data Page
userStories.description: As a user, I want a polished landing page with novel-insight card and transparent data-sources page, so that the tool is credible and easy to enter.
acceptanceCriteria: Precondition: US-004 done (pnpm benchmark exits 0)

Create src/components/site-nav.tsx: responsive nav with links Home (/), Analyse (/analyse), Compare (/compare), History (/history), About (/about). Mobile hamburger below md. Keyboard-accessible focus rings
Create src/components/site-footer.tsx: OGL v3.0 attribution, MIT licence link, GitHub repo link, About link
Modify src/app/layout.tsx: wrap children with SiteNav + SiteFooter. Set <html lang='en'>. Skip-to-content link

Replace src/app/page.tsx with home page: Hero h1 with EXACT tagline 'Know if a London property is a good investment before you buy'; sub-hero paragraph ≥ 80 words covering BTL market context + Section 24 + EPC regulations; postcode search Input with Submit that navigates client-side to /history/[outcode]
Home page novel-insight card calls trpc.analytics.novelInsight via useQuery. Shows shadcn Skeleton during pending. Shows Alert with Retry on error. Renders title, description, value, methodology
Home page 3 CTA tiles (grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3) linking to /analyse /compare /history with Lucide icons (Calculator, Scale, LineChart), title, one-liner, 'Explore →' Button

Create src/app/about/page.tsx. Static config src/config/data-sources.ts with 7 entries: Land Registry, ONS HPI, ONS Rental, data.police.uk, EPC Register, ONS Postcode Directory, Manual Mortgage Products. Each entry: name, url, licence, description, volume (approx)
About page sections: Data Sources list, Methodology (S11 labels + composite score weighting 35/25/20/20), Known Limitations (summarises 5 risk register items), Licence (app MIT, data OGL v3.0)
All interactive elements have visible focus states; images/icons have aria-label; form inputs labelled; heading hierarchy logical
Mobile responsive tested at viewports 375/768/1920 (Tailwind sm:/md:/lg:)

Unit tests: tests/app/home.test.tsx using vitest + @testing-library/react + jsdom. Mock trpc. Tests: (1) hero renders EXACT tagline text; (2) while novelInsight loading, screen contains Skeleton (role=status); (3) when novelInsight resolves, screen contains returned title; (4) 3 CTA links with correct hrefs; (5) submitting valid postcode SE16 7PB navigates to /history/SE16 (mock useRouter, assert push called with '/history/SE16')
Unit tests: tests/app/about.test.tsx: (1) 7 data source entries render; (2) OGL entries show 'Open Government Licence v3.0' text; (3) Methodology heading exists; (4) Limitations contains ≥ 3 items
Deploy: vercel --prod --yes — must return 200 at $PRODUCTION_URL
curl -s $PRODUCTION_URL | grep -q 'Know if a London property' — must match
curl -s $PRODUCTION_URL/about | grep -q 'Open Government Licence v3.0' — must match
Verify: pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm build — all exit 0
Commit: git add -A && git commit -m 'feat(ui): add home page with novel insight card and about-the-data page' && git push
Verify CI green
Typecheck passes: pnpm typecheck

userStories.title: US-006: Property Analyser (What-If Calculator) Page
userStories.description: As a user, I want a form to analyse a hypothetical property and see composite score, comparables, and IO-vs-Repayment mortgage scenarios, so that I can evaluate a specific investment.
acceptanceCriteria: Precondition: US-005 done (home page returns 200)

Create src/lib/mortgage.ts exporting calculateMonthlyPayment(principal: number, annualRatePct: number, termYears: number, mortgageType: 'REPAYMENT'|'INTEREST_ONLY'): number. Logic matches PL/pgSQL function exactly (US-002 verified)

Unit tests: tests/lib/mortgage.test.ts with 5 cases mirroring US-002 DB tests: (1) 300000/5.5/25/REPAYMENT between 1841-1843; (2) 300000/5.5/25/INTEREST_ONLY exactly 1375; (3) 300000/0/25/REPAYMENT exactly 1000; (4) 0/5.5/25/REPAYMENT exactly 0; (5) 500000/4.25/30/REPAYMENT between 2459-2461

Create src/lib/analyse-schema.ts exporting analyseInputSchema: Zod object with postcode regex, bedrooms coerce int 1-6, propertyType enum, askingPrice coerce min 50000 max 10000000, expectedMonthlyRent coerce min 100 max 20000, depositPct coerce min 5 max 50 default 25, mortgageProductId coerce int positive

Create src/app/analyse/page.tsx using React Hook Form + zodResolver(analyseInputSchema). Form fields: postcode (Input), bedrooms (Select 1-6), propertyType (Select 5 enum formatted 'Detached', 'Semi-Detached', 'Terraced', 'Flat', 'Other'), askingPrice (number Input £ prefix), expectedMonthlyRent (number Input £ prefix), depositPct (Slider 5-50 default 25 % suffix), mortgageProductId (Select options from trpc.analytics.listMortgageProducts useQuery)
Form validation errors display inline via react-hook-form fieldState.error.message in text-destructive. Submit button disabled while isSubmitting
On valid submit: call trpc.analytics.compositeScore useMutation + trpc.analytics.comparables useMutation with same postcode/bedrooms/propertyType. floor area defaulted to 70sqm if not provided

Results card (after successful mutation): (1) large Badge with composite score coloured red<40/amber 40-60/green>60; (2) verdict Badge with emoji (💰 CASH_COW / 📈 GROWTH_PLAY / 🦄 UNICORN / ⚠️ AVOID); (3) 4 Progress bars labelled Yield/Capital Growth/Risk (Low Crime + Low Volatility)/Cashflow; (4) grossYieldPct and netYieldPct as stat cards with tooltips

Mortgage scenarios section: two Cards side-by-side 'Interest-Only' and 'Repayment'. Each shows monthly payment (from src/lib/mortgage.ts client calc), total paid over term, remaining balance (0 for repayment, principal for IO). Authoritative server value (from compositeScore response) shown in italics below if differs > £1
Mortgage cashflow Tremor BarChart: rent income, mortgage payment, costs (25% of rent), net cashflow — one group per scenario. aria-label summarising comparison

Comparables table (shadcn Table): columns Area, Sale Date, Sale Price, £/sqft, Bedrooms. Sortable via Tanstack Table or state. Shows ≤ 10 rows sorted by sale_date DESC default. If < 3 comparables: shadcn Alert 'Limited comparables — consider widening criteria'

Empty state before first submit: 'Enter a property above to analyse' with Lucide BarChart3 icon. Error state: shadcn Alert variant=destructive with error message and Retry button re-submitting last valid values
Mobile responsive: form stacks vertically below md; results cards stack; comparables table overflow-x-auto

Unit tests: tests/app/analyse.test.tsx: (1) 7 form fields render with labels; (2) empty postcode submit shows error text containing 'valid UK postcode'; (3) askingPrice=30000 shows validation error; (4) valid submit calls compositeScore mutation with correct input shape (mock via vi.mock); (5) after successful mutation, results card renders with composite score text; (6) mortgage section shows two sub-cards headed 'Interest-Only' and 'Repayment'
Deploy: vercel --prod --yes — must succeed
curl -sI $PRODUCTION_URL/analyse returns 200
Verify: pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm build — all exit 0
Commit: git add -A && git commit -m 'feat(ui): add property analyser with IO vs Repayment mortgage scenarios and composite scoring' && git push
Verify CI green
Typecheck passes: pnpm typecheck

userStories.title: US-007: Compare, Historical Drill-Down, Quadrant, and Choropleth
userStories.description: As a user, I want to compare up to 4 postcodes side-by-side, drill down into area history with nested trend charts, and see a quadrant plot plus crime choropleth, so that I can contextualise investment opportunities visually.
acceptanceCriteria: Precondition: US-006 done

Create src/app/compare/page.tsx: state array of up to 4 postcodes. Each in Card with remove button (Lucide X). 'Add postcode' button disabled at array length 4; 5th attempt shows toast (shadcn sonner) 'Maximum 4 postcodes reached'

When array non-empty: call trpc.analytics.compareSnapshot useQuery with outcodes extracted from postcodes. Results in 4-column grid (grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4), each Card: outcode, median price, avg yield %, yoy growth %, crime index, quadrant Badge

Tremor BarChart below cards showing yield % across outcodes. Nivo ResponsiveScatterPlot: x=yoy_price_growth_pct, y=avg_yield_pct, dots labelled with outcode, quadrant backgrounds via SVG layer (UNICORN top-right lime, CASH_COW bottom-right teal, GROWTH_PLAY top-left sky, AVOID bottom-left red). aria-label summarising data
Create src/app/history/[outcode]/page.tsx dynamic route. Server-render on demand. Calls trpc.analytics.historicalDrilldown({outcode})

History page: 4 stacked Tremor LineCharts (full-width, vertically stacked) titled 'Postcode sector trend', 'Outcode trend', 'Area trend', 'London region trend'. Each plots rolling_12m_avg + yoy_growth_pct. Each chart aria-label describing 12-month trend
History page breadcrumb above charts (shadcn Breadcrumb): Home > Historical > {area} > {outcode}. Links clickable

History page Key Findings section below charts: calls trpc.analytics.historicalFindings returning 2-3 insight strings. If errors/empty: shadcn Alert 'Findings will appear once historical data is loaded'

Add procedure analytics.historicalFindings with Zod input { outcode: z.string() }. SQL: compare latest rolling_12m_avg YoY growth to region avg, price volatility vs region, peak-trough ratio. Returns array of up to 3 { title, description, value }

Create src/app/insights/quadrant/page.tsx: Nivo ResponsiveScatterPlot showing ALL London outcodes colored by quadrant (CASH_COW teal, GROWTH_PLAY sky, UNICORN lime, AVOID red), outcode labels on hover tooltip. Data from trpc.analytics.quadrant. Legend showing 4 quadrant meanings

Download London boroughs GeoJSON via one-off scripts/fetch-geojson.ts from https://data.london.gov.uk/dataset/statistical-gis-boundary-files-london, save to public/geo/london-boroughs.geojson. File must exist, size > 10KB, valid GeoJSON with 'features' array ≥ 20 features

Create src/app/insights/crime-map/page.tsx: Nivo ResponsiveChoropleth reading /geo/london-boroughs.geojson. Fill by INVERTED crime rate (green safer, red more crime). New tRPC procedure analytics.crimeByBorough aggregating crime_stat to borough via location join. alt text summarising top 5 safest + least-safe boroughs
Update src/components/site-nav.tsx: add Insights dropdown with links to /insights/quadrant and /insights/crime-map

Unit tests: tests/app/compare.test.tsx: (1) initial shows one empty card; (2) entering 4 valid postcodes fills 4 cards; (3) 5th attempt shows toast; (4) removing postcode updates count; (5) compareSnapshot called with outcodes. tests/app/history.test.tsx: (1) mocks historicalDrilldown; (2) renders 4 LineChart components with distinct data-testid. tests/components/quadrant-chart.test.tsx: (1) renders 4 quadrant backgrounds; (2) plots dots for data; (3) tooltip shows outcode on hover
Deploy: vercel --prod --yes — must succeed
Smoke tests: curl -sI returns 200 for $PRODUCTION_URL/compare, /history/SE16, /insights/quadrant, /insights/crime-map
Verify: pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm build — all exit 0
Commit: git add -A && git commit -m 'feat(ui): add compare, historical drill-down, quadrant scatter, and crime choropleth pages' && git push
Verify CI green
Typecheck passes: pnpm typecheck

userStories.title: US-008: Quality Gates, Evidence Pack, and Deployment Plan Skeleton
userStories.description: As the user writing the report, I want all automated quality gates passed and all verifiable evidence captured, so that the submission satisfies every automatable A+ criterion.
acceptanceCriteria: Precondition: US-007 done (all 7 routes return 200)
Pre-install Playwright browser: pnpm exec playwright install chromium --with-deps

Write scripts/screenshots.ts per ralph/context/05-verification-scripts.md: Playwright headless Chromium, viewport 1920x1080, deviceScaleFactor 2. Navigate to $PRODUCTION_URL (or localhost:3000 via pnpm start fallback). For each of 7 routes (/, /analyse, /compare, /history/SE16, /about, /insights/quadrant, /insights/crime-map): wait networkidle, full-page PNG to evidence/06-dashboard/0N-<page>.png. For /analyse: fill form (SE16, 2bed, Flat, £400k, £1800, 25%, first mortgage product), submit, capture evidence/06-dashboard/03-property-analyser-results.png. Exits 0 if all captured

Run pnpm screenshots — must exit 0. Verify 8 screenshots exist in evidence/06-dashboard/: 01-landing.png, 02-postcode-explorer.png (or merged with 01), 03-property-analyser.png, 03-property-analyser-results.png, 04-compare.png, 05-historical-drilldown.png, 06-about-data.png, 07-quadrant.png, 08-crime-map.png. Each file size ≥ 50KB. Verify via sharp: each width ≥ 1920

Write scripts/audit-a11y.ts per ralph/context/05-verification-scripts.md: Playwright + @axe-core/playwright. For each of 7 pages: injectAxe + checkA11y with tags wcag2a/wcag2aa/wcag21a/wcag21aa. Aggregate violations to evidence/06-dashboard/axe-audit.json. Exits 0 iff zero violations across all pages

Run pnpm audit:a11y — iterate on violations until zero. Common fixes: aria-labels on icons, contrast ratios, form labels, heading hierarchy, focus rings. Final axe-audit.json must show zero violations across all 7 pages

Write scripts/audit-lighthouse.ts per ralph/context/05-verification-scripts.md: lighthouse npm package + chrome-launcher. Desktop preset. Two runs (warm + scored), keep scored. Save JSON to evidence/06-dashboard/lighthouse-desktop.json. Exits 0 iff Performance/Accessibility/BestPractices/SEO all ≥ 90

Run pnpm audit:lighthouse — iterate on issues until all 4 categories ≥ 90. Common fixes: next/image optimisation, metadata in layout.tsx (title, description, OG image), cache headers, reduce initial JS bundle
Run pnpm benchmark — must exit 0 (p50 < 200ms all 4 queries). Update evidence/06-dashboard/performance-benchmarks.md with final numbers

Write evidence/07-migrations-demo/expand-contract-example.md: worked example for 'renaming property.address_line_1 to property.primary_address_line' with 5-step SQL snippets (ADD COLUMN nullable → backfill → app reads both → app writes both → DROP OLD). Include deployment commentary at each step

Write evidence/06-dashboard/loom-script.md: 4-minute walkthrough SKELETON with timestamps (0:00, 0:15, 0:45, 2:00, 3:00, 3:45, 4:00), section prompts for hook/app tour/live query/novel insight/code/closing. User fills in final voice. File size ≥ 1500 bytes

Write evidence/09-README.md master index: Markdown table with columns File | Description | Report Section | Appendix Number. Every file in evidence/ listed. Includes Live URL, GitHub URL, Loom URL placeholder. Verify: grep -c '.png\|.sql\|.json\|.md' evidence/09-README.md ≥ 30
Write scripts/verify-evidence.ts per ralph/context/05-verification-scripts.md: loops expected files, asserts exists + non-empty + (PNGs) width ≥ 1920 via sharp. Exits 0 iff all pass
Run pnpm verify:evidence — must exit 0

Update README.md: replace placeholder URLs with actual Vercel $PRODUCTION_URL and GitHub https://github.com/harryfgx/yieldlens. Add hero badges: CI status (GitHub Actions badge), Vercel deploy, MIT licence, TypeScript. Add Live Demo + GitHub buttons. Add architecture diagram (ASCII or Mermaid)

docs/deployment-plan.md remains SKELETON with required section headings (Infrastructure Choice / Environments and CI/CD / Backward-Compatible Schema Migrations / Change Management Theory / Security and Integrity Safeguards / Rollback Plan). Verify: grep -c '^## ' docs/deployment-plan.md ≥ 6. User writes the prose
Verify repo public: gh repo view harryfgx/yieldlens --json visibility --jq '.visibility' returns 'PUBLIC'
Verify production stable: curl -sI returns 200 for $PRODUCTION_URL, /analyse, /compare, /about, /insights/quadrant, /insights/crime-map
Final comprehensive verification: pnpm verify:all && pnpm benchmark && pnpm audit:a11y && pnpm audit:lighthouse && pnpm verify:data-quality && pnpm verify:evidence — all exit 0
Commit: git add -A && git commit -m 'chore: final quality gates, evidence pack, and deployment plan skeleton' && git push
Sleep 90; verify CI green: gh run list --workflow ci.yml --limit 1 --json conclusion --jq '.[0].conclusion' returns 'success'
Typecheck passes: pnpm typecheck

userStories.title: US-009: Harvard Reference Verification Tooling
userStories.description: As the user writing the report, I want programmatic reference verification tooling so that once I populate docs/references.md, every citation is truth-checked against real sources.
acceptanceCriteria: Precondition: US-008 done

Write scripts/verify-references.ts: parses docs/references.md (skeleton from US-001). For each entry: (a) books → HTTP GET https://www.googleapis.com/books/v1/volumes?q=isbn:<ISBN> OR ?q=intitle:<title>+inauthor:<author>, assert response body contains cited title + author (case-insensitive substring); (b) journal articles → https://api.crossref.org/works/<doi>; (c) industry reports → web search for organisation + year; (d) seminal papers → CrossRef / Google Scholar snippet. Writes evidence/10-references/verification-report.json with {reference, verification_url, verified, evidence_snippet}. Exits 0 iff every reference verified=true. If docs/references.md is still skeleton (empty reference entries), exits 0 with message 'No references to verify yet — populate docs/references.md'

Write scripts/check-citations.ts: greps docs/*.md for common cited author surnames (Codd, Chen, Porter, Elmasri, Beaulieu, Laplante, Vlasceanu, Loshin, Knaflic, Wexler, Vergadia, Rosen, Fernando) and asserts each appears in docs/references.md when cited. Exits 0 iff consistent
Add pnpm verify:references script to package.json invoking scripts/verify-references.ts
Add 'verify:references' job to .github/workflows/ci.yml so future changes to references trigger verification
Create evidence/10-references/ directory (ensure exists from US-001)
Update evidence/09-README.md master index: add rows for evidence/10-references/verification-report.json with description and report-section mapping

Unit tests: tests/scripts/verify-references.test.ts with 3 cases: (1) valid book reference returns verified=true when mocked Google Books API returns matching title; (2) invalid book returns verified=false; (3) empty references list returns immediate success with message
Write scripts-docs: docs/reference-verification.md explaining how to use (populate references.md → run pnpm verify:references → fix any flagged)
Verify: pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm build — all exit 0
Commit: git add -A && git commit -m 'feat(refs): add Harvard reference verification tooling with CrossRef and Google Books checks' && git push
Verify CI green
Typecheck passes: pnpm typecheck
